name: Survey — Context Engineering for LLMs (arXiv 2507.13334)
context_primitives: July 2025 survey (Mei et al.) analyzing 1400+ papers; formalizes **Context Engineering** beyond prompts into: (1) retrieval/generation, (2) processing (long sequence, refinement, structure), (3) **management** (memory hierarchies, compression, optimization). Systems layer covers RAG variants, **memory systems**, tool-integrated reasoning, multi-agent orchestration. Flags **comprehension–generation gap**: models ingest long context better than they produce equally long coherent output.
deployment_pattern: Use taxonomy to classify your stack: Alex = management (compression) + multi-agent + tool reasoning on observability data.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Survey synthesizes benchmark families (long-context, RAG, agent); does not replace product-specific long-session evals.
ecosystem_integrations: Awesome-Context-Engineering GitHub companion repo cited by authors.
differentiators: Academic **map** vs. Alex **point solution**: survey validates that **memory hierarchies and compression** are mainstream research themes—Alex's head/tail+store is one instance of management; Mem0/Zep are memory-system implementations.
significance_for_builders: When pitching architecture, place Alex tactics under **Context Management** and **Multi-Agent Systems** chapters of this survey—not as ad hoc hacks.

primary_sources.title: A Survey of Context Engineering for Large Language Models
